title: Supply Chain Operator (all genders)
required_skills: Completed degree (economics, logistics, engineering or related)
Proficiency in BI tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or Google Looker Studio
Familiarity with flowcharts and risk analysis
Clear and confident communication in German and English
matched_skills: Power BI (PL-300 certified) and Google Looker Studio — directly satisfies BI tools requirement
Completed degree (Master of Management & Entrepreneurship, Leuphana) — satisfies education requirement
Fluent English (C1) and German (B1) — partially satisfies language requirement
Cross-functional project coordination and milestone documentation at HSBC and Hapag-Lloyd — partial match for planning and reporting responsibilities
Risk escalation and prioritisation management at HSBC — partial match for risk analysis requirement
missing_skills: No supply chain management experience whatsoever — this is the core operational domain
No demand planning, supplier qualification, or quality management experience
German B1 is below the clear and confident standard expected; aerospace client site requires strong German
core_responsibilities: Manage cross-cutting planning processes and synchronise stakeholder objectives
Analyse existing supply chain processes and develop improvement measures; implement changes
Coordinate with customers and suppliers; assess supplier qualifications
Document project milestones and produce meaningful reporting with data
recommended_emphasis: Highlight Power BI and Looker Studio credentials prominently — these are explicitly listed in the JD
Frame the HSBC programme coordination (6 workstreams, risk escalation) as cross-functional planning process management
Be transparent that supply chain and aerospace domain knowledge is lacking — Accenture hiring managers will probe this directly
If applying, position the candidate as a junior Operations/BI generalist rather than a supply chain specialist
summary: Weak match at 38.5%. The BI tools requirement is well-covered (Power BI PL-300, Looker Studio), but the role is fundamentally a supply chain operations position requiring domain expertise in logistics, demand planning, and supplier management — areas where the candidate has zero background. The aerospace context adds further specificity. Only advisable if applying to the Accenture Operations track broadly and accepting the supply chain domain as a learning curve.
